page: Amazon Web Services i3.16xlarge is a storage-optimized instance powered by the Xen hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon E5-2686 v4 processor with 64 vCPUs and 32 physical cores operating at a clock speed of 2.3 GHz. The x86_64 architecture includes 45 MB of L3 cache and supports AVX, AES, and FMA instruction sets.

The instance is equipped with 488.0 GB of system memory, providing 7.62 GB per core. A key hardware feature is its bundled local storage, consisting of 15,200 GB of NVMe SSD capacity. Network connectivity is supported by a 25 Gbps baseline bandwidth. No GPU accelerators are present on this instance type.

Performance benchmarks indicate a clear tradeoff between single-core and multi-core workloads. Single-core CPU performance, single-threaded gzip compression, and single-core Geekbench scores fall into the bottom 10% of tested servers. However, multi-core CPU performance, multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, and Passmark CPU scores reach average levels. Memory bandwidth and database operations also perform in the middle tier.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the i3.16xlarge offers high local storage density and substantial memory capacity relative to its compute resources, making it a viable option for storage-heavy deployments without requiring external block storage. This hardware profile is best suited for large-scale databases, NoSQL data stores, transactional systems, and data-intensive workloads that require high local NVMe capacity and moderate multi-core processing power.
description: Amazon Web Services i3.16xlarge is a storage-optimized instance designed for data-intensive workloads. Powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2686 v4 processor, it provides 64 vCPUs, 32 physical cores, and 488.0 GB of memory. The instance features a substantial local storage capacity of 15,200 GB delivered via NVMe SSDs, alongside a 25 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark data reveals a performance tradeoff: single-core CPU execution is relatively weak, placing in the bottom 10%, whereas multi-core operations, memory bandwidth, and database transactions perform in the average tier. This resource profile offers high storage density and memory capacity, making it qualitatively cost-effective for storage-heavy deployments. The i3.16xlarge is highly suited for large-scale databases, NoSQL systems, and transactional workloads requiring massive local NVMe storage and moderate multi-threaded compute capabilities.
og_description: Amazon Web Services i3.16xlarge is a storage-optimized instance featuring 64 vCPUs, 488 GB of RAM, and 15,200 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, designed for large-scale database and transactional workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services i3.16xlarge is a storage-optimized instance with 64 vCPUs, 488 GB RAM, and 15,200 GB of local NVMe SSD storage.
tagline: A storage-optimized platform featuring massive local NVMe SSD capacity and balanced memory for data-intensive database workloads.

488.0 GB of system memory with 7.62 GB per core
15,200 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage
Average multi-core and database performance contrasted with weak single-core metrics
Best suited for large-scale databases, NoSQL systems, and transactional workloads
